NR
About Insights Free Tool The Audit
Private brief · Prepared for Nathan Kam, Anthology FINN Partners · May 2026

AI now answers where to stay, where to fly, what to do in Hawaii. Right now gohawaii.com and hawaiianairlines.com are not in the answer. Here is the data, the fix, and what an agency partnership with Anthology FINN could look like.

00 Why this brief exists

This brief is private and prepared for Anthology FINN's tourism book. It exists because in 2026, AI engines decide where Hawaii visitors stay, fly, and spend their time. Most "where should I stay," "what should I do in Hawaii," "best flight to Maui," and "is Waikiki safe right now" decisions now run through an AI step before they hit a booking page. Someone planning from the mainland, someone arriving at HNL, someone deciding between activities the morning of, asks Siri or ChatGPT or Perplexity. Each business named in the answer was chosen by structured data the engines can read. The rest are not in the answer at all.

Two of Anthology FINN's most visible tourism clients (gohawaii.com and hawaiianairlines.com) are not in the answer right now. Both scored an F on our AI search readiness audit yesterday. The actual scan data is in section 02 and is reproducible in 30 seconds at check.neverranked.com.

The category is new, so the brief is long. What follows: what is at stake for the tourism book, the scan data on those two clients, why Hawaii tourism is the right starting market for AEO, the local proof case (Hawaii Theatre, 45 to 95 in ten days), what an engagement looks like for one client, and what an agency partnership with Anthology FINN could look like in practice.

01 What is at stake for Hawaii tourism right now

Three behavior shifts in the last twelve months turn this from "nice to have" into "your clients are already losing intent traffic they cannot see."

  • Apple Intelligence and Perplexity in Safari are default-on for millions of iPhone users planning Hawaii trips. Their first question is "best place to stay in Maui," "things to do in Waikiki," "is Hanauma Bay open." The AI returns one or two specific businesses, bureaus, or activity providers. The rest of the answer is structured data the engines trust. The companies not in that data are not in the answer.
  • Google AI Overviews surface above the blue links for every "best Hawaii vacation" type query. HVCB might rank well on Google and still be invisible if the AI Overview names a competing destination site.
  • ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini include web-grounded search by default. People plan honeymoons, family trips, and conference travel using these tools, and they get cited brand names. We track this directly across seven engines: ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Claude, Microsoft Copilot, Google AI Overviews, and Gemma.

None of this shows up in your client's Google Analytics. It is real intent traffic that walks past the brand without registering as a missed click. The only way a client influences whether they appear in an AI answer is by publishing structured data the engines trust. That is the whole game.

02 The actual scan data on two of your clients, yesterday

Rather than walk through generic best practices, here is what our scanner returned on two of Anthology FINN's most visible clients. These scans are reproducible. Drop either URL into check.neverranked.com and you will get the same numbers in 30 seconds.

Client 01

gohawaii.com · Hawaii Visitors and Convention Bureau

35/100
Grade F

Zero JSON-LD schema blocks on the homepage. No Organization markup, no WebSite schema, no BreadcrumbList, no AggregateRating, no FAQ. The official Hawaii tourism site is invisible to AI engines as an entity. When ChatGPT or Perplexity is asked "official guide to Hawaii vacation" or "Hawaii travel information," it has no structured way to recognize gohawaii.com as the authoritative source. Six red flags total.

  • No Organization-type schema
  • No WebSite schema (SearchAction rich results disabled)
  • No BreadcrumbList schema
  • No AggregateRating
  • No og:image tag
  • Title 80 chars (will truncate in SERPs)
Client 02

hawaiianairlines.com · Hawaiian Airlines

25/100
Grade F

Same picture, worse score. Zero JSON-LD schema. No Organization, no WebSite, no AggregateRating. Nine words of indexable text above the fold. When a mainland traveler asks Perplexity "best flight to Honolulu" or "most reliable Hawaii airline," the engines have no structured signal to anchor on. The brand exists; the structured data the engines need to confidently cite the brand does not.

  • No Organization or LocalBusiness schema
  • No WebSite schema
  • No AggregateRating (despite a known-strong brand)
  • No og:image tag
  • Only 9 words of plain-text content on the page above JavaScript boundary
  • Only 1 outbound link (weak authority signal)

The pattern across the rest of your tourism book is almost certainly the same. We did not cherry-pick these two. They were the first two we ran. If you want to send a third (Hyatt Waikiki, Hawaii Tourism Authority, Kauai Visitors Bureau, your pick), I will run it and add it to this brief before we meet.

03 The highest-leverage first fix: claim Bing for Business

Before any schema work, before any content drafting, there is one move worth running on both gohawaii.com and hawaiianairlines.com. Claim their Bing for Business profiles.

This sounds like 2018 advice. It is not. As of 2024, ChatGPT routes most of its web-grounded queries through Bing's index. Microsoft Copilot grounds on Bing directly. Bing's verified business listings are functionally part of the AI engine pipeline for any question in the client's category. An unclaimed Bing profile means:

  • Bing's local pack returns scraped, unverified info or skips the business entirely
  • ChatGPT, knowing Bing trusts unclaimed listings less, often skips those businesses in answers
  • Apple Intelligence pulls from a different surface (Apple Business Connect) but the same dynamic applies

For Hawaiian Airlines, this surfaces on queries like "best airline to Hawaii," "Hawaiian Airlines reviews," "Hawaiian Airlines flight changes," "Lihue to Maui Hawaiian." Bing-friendly question shapes that ChatGPT and Copilot route through Bing.

For gohawaii.com, the surface is "Hawaii vacation planning," "best time to visit Hawaii," "Hawaii travel rules," "official Hawaii tourism site." Authority queries where Bing's local pack and the AI-engine summaries pull from the verified listing.

We walked Hawaii Theatre Center through claiming their Bing profile this week. The honest framing going in (and this is the framing every Hawaii business operates under right now): the listing felt like a low-priority site because the team assumed nobody used it. That framing is from the pre-AI era. It is the reason this surface is currently uncrowded.

What it actually takes
Submit the profile (basics: name, address, phone, website, email, categories)~20 minutes
Bing mails a verification postcard5-10 business days
Enter verification code on arrivalsame day, attention permitting
Listing publishes after code entry7-12 days
Total submission to live listing~3 weeks

Slower than Google Business Profile, which often turns around in 1-3 days for an established business. Worth setting that expectation up front so the client is not surprised at week two.

Two leverage moves inside the flow

  • Import from Google. If the client already has a Google Business Profile, Bing's submission flow offers a one-click import that pre-fills name, address, phone, website, and categories. Cuts the 20-minute manual entry to about 5 minutes, and surfaces any Google-Bing discrepancies. Always start here when GBP exists.
  • Multi-category selection. Bing allows 3-5 categories per profile. Most clients pick only the most obvious primary and stop. Each additional category adds eligible query coverage. Specific recommendations for the two clients above:
    • Hawaiian Airlines: Airline (primary) plus Travel services plus Airport plus Transportation
    • gohawaii.com: Tourism information center (primary) plus Government office plus Travel services

One warning

Bing mails the verification postcard to the exact address the client submits. If the address is wrong, the postcard never arrives, and the business loops back to the start of a 3-week clock. Verify the mailing address matches public listings before recommending submission.

The framing in one paragraph. Bing for Business profile claim is the highest-leverage, lowest-effort AI-citation surface fix available in 2026. Free, about 20 minutes of active work, about 3 weeks to a published listing, and a meaningful lift in ChatGPT and Microsoft Copilot citations on category and brand queries. Most SEO commentary still treats Bing as a tier-2 search engine. That framing is from the pre-AI era and is the reason this surface is currently uncrowded.

04 Why Hawaii is the right starting market

Hawaii tourism is the right starting market for AEO and Anthology FINN is the right agency to run it. National AEO work for a destination brand competes against thousands of well-structured properties. Hawaii has a finite, named set of major tourism players, and almost none of them have proper structured data yet. The first three or four clients you bring onto monitoring (HVCB, HTA, Hawaiian Airlines, the Hyatt, take your pick) will see citation share shift inside ninety days. There is not a long tail to fight through.

Visitor intent amplifies this. Every traveler planning a Hawaii trip is asking AI engines questions Anthology FINN's clients should be the answer to. "Best hotel in Waikiki for a couples trip." "Hawaii activities for families with teens." "Direct flights to Lihue." "Is it safe to swim at Hanauma Bay right now." The clients in the AI answer get the consideration. The rest get skipped, and nothing in standard Google Analytics will tell them why.

You already know which of your clients move fast on emerging channels and which need three rounds of internal approval before they will say yes to anything new. That kind of judgment is what turns an agency partnership into a defensible book, not a one-off deployment.

05 Why NeverRanked, honestly

Most AEO tools tell you what to do. We do it. As of May 2026, NeverRanked is the only AEO platform we have evaluated that ships all of the following together:

  • Seven-engine citation tracking: Perplexity, ChatGPT, Gemini, Google AI Overviews (live via DataForSEO), Claude, Microsoft Copilot via Bing, and Gemma (Google's open-weight model, added May 2026). No other AEO platform tracks all seven. Profound and Athena cover three to four. Ahrefs Brand Radar covers four.
  • Done-for-you schema deployment via a one-line snippet. No CMS access, no dev cycle. Profound, Athena HQ, Ahrefs Brand Radar all stop at "here is what to deploy." We deploy it.
  • Multi-pass AI validation on every generated output. Factual grounding against the source, tone check, quality gate, three-attempt regeneration. Most "AI content" tools ship Claude's first draft. We refuse to.
  • Variant impact attribution: every schema we deploy is tracked as a variant. Citation rate measured 4 weeks before deploy vs 4 weeks after, two-proportion z-test, statistical confidence at p < 0.05. Your monthly Visibility Index includes a "what we deployed and what it did" section. Nobody else does this.
  • Prominence and sentiment within citations, not just yes/no. Where in the AI's answer were you mentioned (rank 1 hero quote vs rank 8 footnote)? Was the tone positive or negative? That's the next layer of AEO that most platforms have not built yet.
  • Real-time citation alerts on Signal+: gained or lost citations between monthly reports get emailed within hours, color-coded by gain or loss. You hear about movement before your competitors do.

Honest framing. NeverRanked is bootstrapped, founder-led, and currently has a small customer count. That is also the partnership advantage. Founder access. Roadmap input. Pricing locked in at the tier you start at. As the platform grows, the firms that came in early have the trust position.

06 What an engagement looks like for one tourism client

Customer-side first, so you can see what an Anthology FINN client would actually receive. The 90-day window is not a sales tactic. AI models retrain on a cycle. Schema deployed in month one gets absorbed into the next training window and surfaces as citations in month three. Anyone promising AI citations in week two is selling theater.

What the engagement looks like, concretely:

  • Days 1-2. We deliver the full audit. The client gets a 90-day implementation roadmap, a competitor citation matrix (which Hawaii tourism players currently win the queries that matter, named, with the threads and pages they get cited from), and a schema fix list with exact JSON-LD code their dev or CMS can paste in.
  • Days 3-30. Schema fixes ship via our one-line snippet. Organization and WebSite schema deployed. LocalBusiness or TouristDestination or Hotel schema as appropriate. AggregateRating published. FAQPage covering the questions visitors actually ask. Each schema runs through our 3-pass validation (factual, tone, quality) before it touches the live site. Every variant we deploy is tracked so we can prove citation lift over time.
  • Days 31-90. AI engines start absorbing the structured data. We track citation share weekly across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Google AI Overviews, Claude, Microsoft Copilot, and Gemma on the queries that matter for the client's bookings or attendance or air travel. You and your client see who they are now appearing alongside, who is still beating them, and real-time citation alerts when a query gains or loses them in any engine. Monthly Visibility Index PDF delivered the first business day of each month.
  • Day 90 onward. Dashboard refreshes weekly. Quarterly drift detection re-evaluates the landscape so the work does not go stale. The Visibility Index includes a "what we deployed and what it did" section showing citation lift attributable to each schema variant, with statistical confidence at p < 0.05.

There are three monthly tiers after the audit (entry, mid, and full-content) and an agency-partnership rate underneath all of them. I would rather walk you through the structure in person than put numbers on a page that may not match your book. Either way, the audit credits toward whichever tier the client picks.

07 What an agency partnership with Anthology FINN could look like

The customer-facing engagement above is one tourism client. The agency partnership is Anthology FINN bringing several clients into that flow under your brand and your account lead. In plain language:

  • You introduce. You send us a client. We do not contact them without you. Always.
  • We onboard inside 48 hours. Schema grader, dashboard, citation tracking, baseline. Your client sees Anthology FINN on the dashboard, not NeverRanked.
  • We execute the work. Schema fixes auto-pushed to their site via the one-line snippet, weekly citation tracking, quarterly drift refresh, authority audits, citation-shaped content drafts when the tier supports it. Every recommendation is visible in the dashboard, so nothing is hidden from your client and nothing is hidden from you.
  • You forward the Monday digest. Plain English, forward-ready under your brand. What changed last week, what mattered most, what we recommend next. You stay the account lead. You bill the client. We bill you.

The economics: three tiers, a per-client agency rate underneath retail on each one, monthly billing, no annual lock. The agency rate kicks in on the first active client and improves with volume. I would rather walk through specific numbers in person when I know more about your book and what tier mix actually makes sense. The shape of the math is straightforward and the rate is built so a partnership is materially better for both sides than direct sales would be.

One real constraint to flag: the full-content tier (the one where we draft and publish citation-shaped content in the client's voice) is capped globally because the human-curated side does not scale infinitely. If you bring clients you want on that tier, claiming the slots earlier is better than later.

08 Why us, briefly
  • We built the tools, then ran them on ourselves first. Schema grader, citation tracker, drift detection, authority audits, the public schema scorer at https://check.neverranked.com?utm_source=pitch&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=anthology-finn. Same software that audited gohawaii.com and hawaiianairlines.com above is what runs on our own domain.
  • The codebase is public at github.com/LanceRoylo/neverranked. You or any developer at Anthology FINN can read every line of what gets injected into a client site. The MCP server we published this week (the first AEO toolchain in the agent ecosystem) is in the same repo: npmjs.com/package/@neverranked/mcp.
  • Security details are public too. The schema injection snippet appends JSON-LD to the head and does nothing else. No cookies, no DOM read, no outbound calls. Full breakdown at neverranked.com/security.
  • We are local. Hello Again Coffee is my shop on Nuuanu Avenue. The Hawaii businesses we audit are places we know exist and people we have met. That matters in a market this small.
09 The local proof point

The fastest way to see the kind of citation lift we are describing: Hawaii Theatre Center, the historic Honolulu venue we work with. They came to us with a 45 out of 100 AEO score and zero AI citations across queries about Honolulu venues. Ten days later, after we deployed five schema categories (PerformingArtsTheater, WebSite, AggregateRating, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList plus 35 auto-refreshing Event schemas), Hawaii Theatre scored a 95 and Perplexity named them on 14 of 19 tracked queries. For Anthology FINN's tourism clients, the equivalent schema package is Organization + WebSite + LocalBusiness or TouristDestination or Hotel + AggregateRating + FAQPage with the questions visitors actually ask. Same playbook, different schema palette. Full case study with the numbers.

10 Two ways to take the next step

Come by Hello Again Coffee.
It is my shop on Nuuanu Avenue. Bring two or three Anthology FINN clients you would want to see scanned live. We will pull a citation share report on each, walk through the schema gaps that surface, and talk about what an agency partnership could look like in practice. Coffee is on me.

Reply with a date and I will make it work →
Mornings, afternoons, or after-hours all work. If a remote walkthrough fits better than coming by the shop, I can put a screen share on the calendar instead. Either is fine.

Either way, thank you for reading this far.
